literal: general-mark, universal-characteristic; from sāmānya 'universal, general' + lakṣaṇa 'mark, characteristic'
invariant_definition: *Sāmānya-lakṣaṇa* is the Dignāga–Dharmakīrti term for the universal-as-conceptually-constructed — the proper object of *anumāna* (inference) and of every concept-bearing cognition. Paired with *svalakṣaṇa* (the bare causally-efficacious particular, proper object of *pratyakṣa*), it supplies the load-bearing distinction in the Buddhist epistemological account of cognition. The *sāmānya-lakṣaṇa* is *not* a real *jāti* inhering in particulars by *samavāya* (against the Naiyāyika); it is a conceptually-constructed grouping whose apparent positive content is the result of the *apoha* (exclusion-function) operating on the substrate of the *svalakṣaṇa*-flow. The criterion of its status is *artha-kriyā*: the *sāmānya-lakṣaṇa* lacks determinate causal efficacy (the universal cowness does not give milk; only *this* cow gives milk) and is therefore *saṃvṛti-sat* rather than *paramārtha-sat*.

per_school.definition: Foundational doctrine. The proper object of *anumāna* and of every concept-bearing cognition. Conceptually-constructed via *apoha*; lacks determinate causal efficacy; *saṃvṛti-sat* in the two-truths register. Dharmakīrti develops the position with the *vāsanā*-grounded *apoha* analysis: the apparent positive content of universal-cognition is generated by the karmic-disposition shaping of cognitive flow, not by any positive *jāti*-reality.

Refused on grounds of inadequacy. The Naiyāyika *jāti-sāmānya* is a real category-entity inhering in the particulars by *samavāya*, and the *sāmānya*-cognition delivered in *savikalpaka-pratyakṣa* and in *anumāna* tracks this real *jāti*. The Buddhist reduction of *sāmānya* to *apoha*-constructed *sāmānya-lakṣaṇa* is, on the Naiyāyika reading, an evasion that fails to account for the determinate-content of *jāti*-cognition.

The Advaita Vedānta-Paribhāṣā and the Mādhva *Pramāṇa-Paddhati* both treat *sāmānya* as a real category in their own *padārtha*-systems, implicitly siding with the Naiyāyika rather than the Buddhist analysis. The Buddhist account of *sāmānya-lakṣaṇa* is engaged principally through the Naiyāyika reply.

translator_note: 'Universal' is the standard gloss, but the term is technical and the standard gloss carries the misleading suggestion that *sāmānya-lakṣaṇa* names something like the Naiyāyika *jāti*. The Buddhist position is precisely that there is no such positive entity — *sāmānya-lakṣaṇa* is a conceptually-constructed grouping. 'Universal-characteristic' or 'general-mark' preserves the technical force more accurately.
